visa_on_arrival.notes: 3 months, multiple entry; available at Turkish ports of entry
3 months, multiple entry; available at Turkish ports of entry

e_visa.notes: E-visa eligible; age restrictions apply for ordinary passport holders ages 15–64. Travelers under 15 or over 65 may be visa-free for 90 days under age-based exemption (see conditional_access).
3 months, multiple entry; also eligible for visa on arrival
Ordinary passport holders eligible for e-visa
3 months, multiple entry; also eligible for visa on arrival

e_visa.nationality: Greek Cypriot Administration of Southern Cyprus
e_visa.notes: E-visa required; visa issuance at borders terminated January 2, 2026. Must obtain e-visa online before travel.
30 days, single entry e-visa for ordinary passport holders. Diplomatic passport holders are visa-free.

visa_policy.notes: Visa-free stays generally capped at 90 days within any 180-day period unless otherwise noted (Russia: 60 days per entry/90 days total; Indonesia/Thailand/Mongolia/Iran/Costa Rica/Brunei/Belarus/Macao: 30 days per entry). 21+ countries may enter Turkey with national ID card only (no passport required). E-visa obtained online at evisa.gov.tr. Many e-visa nationalities require a valid Schengen, US, UK, or Ireland visa/residence permit as a precondition. The 90-day limit applies regardless of visa type. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from arrival date with 60 days beyond duration of stay. Australia became visa-free April 17, 2026 (presidential decree). China became visa-free January 2, 2026. Croatia moved from e-visa to visa-free list. Canada became visa-free since early 2024. United Kingdom (full British Citizen passport) is visa-free for 90 days. Albania, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, San Marino, Sweden confirmed visa-free on MFA page June 2026. North Macedonia (MKD) and Uzbekistan (UZB) added June 2026 (visa-free 90 days). Tajikistan visa exemption cancelled April 2024; Saint Kitts and Nevis cancelled May 2025; Turkmenistan cancelled September 2022.

visa_types.name: e-Visa (Electronic Visa)
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of 100+ eligible countries. Single-entry most common; multiple-entry available for some nationalities. 30-day max stay per entry within 180-day validity. USD 75 standard fee (fee varies by nationality). Applied online at evisa.gov.tr. Only valid at designated entry points.

visa_types.name: Sticker Visa (Consulate Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, business, study, work - for nationalities not eligible for e-Visa
visa_types.notes: Physical sticker visa issued by Turkish consulates/embassies. Applied in person or through authorized VFS centers. Required for nationalities not eligible for e-Visa. Processing time and fee vary by nationality and consulate. Multiple-entry and longer validity available.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Çalışma İzni)
visa_types.notes: Issued by Ministry of Labour and Social Security. Employer-sponsored application. Initial permit 1 year, extendable to 3 years then indefinite. Foreign worker quota rules: generally max 1 foreigner per 5 Turkish employees. Online application via calismaizni.gov.tr. Applicant must apply within Turkey on a valid residence permit or from abroad.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Full-time study at Turkish educational institutions
visa_types.notes: For study programs at Turkish universities and language schools. Requires official acceptance letter from a registered Turkish educational institution. Applied at Turkish embassy/consulate. Upon arrival, student must register with authorities and obtain student residence permit.

visa_types.name: Turkish Citizenship by Investment
visa_types.purpose: Citizenship via real estate or capital investment
visa_types.notes: Routes: (1) Real estate investment USD 400,000+ (held 3 years); (2) Capital investment USD 500,000+ in Turkish company or government bonds; (3) Employment creation (50+ employees). Citizenship granted directly - no residency period required. Application filed with General Directorate of Migration Management. Citizenship confers Turkish passport and full rights. Dual citizenship permitted.

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Schengen visa or residence permit (previously used or unused single-entry visas accepted; e-visas of other countries not accepted; must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.

Holder of a valid Schengen residence permit (must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye; e-visas of other countries not accepted). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.

Holder of a valid US visa (previously used or unused single-entry visas accepted; e-visas of other countries not accepted; must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.
Holder of a valid US residence permit (must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.

Holder of a valid UK visa (previously used or unused single-entry visas accepted; e-visas of other countries not accepted; must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.
Holder of a valid UK residence permit (must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.

Holder of a valid Ireland visa (previously used or unused single-entry visas accepted; e-visas of other countries not accepted; must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.
Holder of a valid Ireland residence permit (must be valid by date of entry into Türkiye). Egypt: ages 15–45 only. Iraq: ages 15–50 only. Libya: ages 16–45 only. Single entry, 1 month.

British National (Overseas) passport holders eligible for e-visa. This applies only to holders of a BN(O) passport, not the ordinary Hong Kong SAR passport (which has separate visa-free access) or ordinary UK British Citizen passports (which are visa-free).
Diplomatic passport holders exempted from visa for stays up to 90 days. Ordinary, special, and service passport holders require visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Official/service and ordinary require visa.
Diplomatic passports exempt for 90 days within any 180-day period. Special, service, and ordinary passport holders must obtain visas.
Members of official delegations holding diplomatic, service, or special passports receive 90-day exemption. Other passport types require visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days within 180 days. Service/ordinary require visa.
Diplomatic passports exempt for 90 days. Official/service and ordinary holders need visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Ordinary/special/service require visa.
Diplomatic holders exempt for 90 days within 180 days. Ordinary/official require visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Official/service and ordinary holders require visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Ordinary and official passport holders must obtain visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Official/service and ordinary holders need visa.
Diplomatic and service passport holders exempt for 90 days within 180 days. Ordinary require visa.
Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Ordinary and official/service passport holders require visa.
Diplomatic holders exempt for 90 days. Official/service/ordinary require visa.

Diplomatic passport holders exempt for 90 days. Official/service and ordinary holders need visa. Note: ordinary Senegalese passport holders may also obtain e-visa with valid Schengen/US/UK/Ireland visa or residence permit.
Diplomatic and service passport holders exempt for 90 days within 180 days. Ordinary require visa.
Diplomatic holders exempt for 90 days within any 180-day period. Ordinary and official holders require visa.

Algerian nationals under age 15 or over age 65 are visa-free for 90 days within 180 days for tourism purposes. Ordinary passport holders aged 15–64 must use the e-visa route (with supporting Schengen/US/UK/Ireland credential).
Egyptian nationals under age 15 or over age 45 are visa-free. Those aged 15–45 must use the credential-based e-visa route.
Iraqi nationals under age 15 or over age 50 are visa-free. Those aged 15–50 must use the credential-based e-visa route.
Libyan nationals under age 16 or over age 45 are visa-free. Those aged 16–45 must use the credential-based e-visa route.

options.notes: Acquiring property worth minimum USD 400,000 (or equivalent foreign currency) with title deed restriction on resale for at least three years; attested by Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change
Fixed capital investment of minimum USD 500,000 or equivalent foreign currency; verified by Ministry of Industry and Technology
Creating employment for at least 50 people; confirmed by Ministry of Labor and Social Security
Bank deposit of minimum USD 500,000 in Turkish banks with three-year non-withdrawal condition; attested by Banking Regulation and Supervision Agency
Purchase of minimum USD 500,000 in government bonds with three-year holding requirement; attested by Ministry of Treasury and Finance
Acquiring minimum USD 500,000 in real estate investment fund or venture capital fund shares; three-year non-sale condition; attested by Capital Markets Board
Contributing minimum USD 500,000 to private pension funds approved by Insurance and Private Pension Regulation and Supervision Agency; three-year minimum holding

cbi.processing_time: Not specified on official page; decision rests with the President of the Republic of Turkey
cbi.notes: Officially called 'exceptional naturalisation' under Turkish Citizenship Law. Residency permit not required as a precondition; property buyers receive renewable short-term residence permits automatically. All investment routes require attestation by relevant ministry. Dual citizenship is permitted under Turkish law (Law No. 5901). Source: invest.gov.tr (Republic of Turkey Investment Office, official government body). Real estate threshold confirmed still at USD 400,000 as of June 2026 (unchanged from September 2018 regulations).

programs.name: Short-Term Residence Permit (Investor)
programs.notes: Investors meeting CBI investment thresholds (e.g. min USD 400,000 real estate, or USD 500,000 other routes) receive short-term residence permit valid up to 5 years (renewable). After 8 years continuous residence a long-term (indefinite) residence permit may be obtained. Investors may also apply directly for citizenship. Source: invest.gov.tr

programs.name: Long-Term Residence Permit
programs.notes: Requires 8 years of continuous legal residence in Turkey. Granted for indefinite period. Holders receive rights comparable to Turkish citizens except voting and military service. Source: en.goc.gov.tr (Presidency of Migration Management)

programs.name: Digital Nomad Identification Certificate / Digital Nomad Visa
programs.eligibility: Ages 21-55; university degree; documented digital/remote work; minimum income of USD 3,000/month (USD 36,000/year); passport valid 6+ months; available to citizens of 34 specified countries (primarily European nations plus USA, Canada, selected former Soviet states)
programs.notes: goturkiye.com is operated by the Turkish Tourism Promotion and Development Agency (TGA), an official government body. Applicants first obtain a Digital Nomad Identification Certificate online, then apply for the visa at a Turkish consulate. Launched April 2024. Holders may subsequently apply for a short-term residence permit via e-ikamet.goc.gov.tr.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the Turkish MFA's Turkish-language outbound visa page for Turkish citizens (ordinary passport) at mfa.gov.tr. Visa-free counts include ordinary passport holders; some entries (Pakistan, diplomatic-only) are limited to official/diplomatic passports. South Korea requires K-ETA pre-travel authorisation. Several countries listed under both visa_free and eta (Indonesia, Seychelles, Thailand) where the entry mechanism has shifted to electronic authorisation but no prior consular visa is required. Kuwait listed as free border-stamp (no fee, 90 days). Iraq access is age-restricted (30 days for those under 15 or over 49; others require visa). Page carries no explicit 'as of' date; accessed June 2026.
